QF.id: Income, Residential Status, Five Heads of Income_17

question.text: Under section 80CCD(2), what is the maximum employer contribution to NPS that is deductible from the employee's income under the NEW tax regime?

answer.text: Employer NPS contribution is not deductible under the new regime

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under section 80CCD(2), employer contribution to NPS is deductible up to 10% of salary under the old tax regime and up to 14% of salary under the new tax regime. This is one of the few deductions still available under the new regime, and the limit is actually MORE generous than the old regime. **The Trap:** Candidates often memorize only the 10% figure or assume that the new regime disallows all deductions. The enhanced 14% limit under the new regime is a deliberate incentive to encourage NPS adoption. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) tests NPS basics; Series X-B tests the regime-specific deduction limits for employer NPS contributions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For clients on the new tax regime, employer NPS contribution at 14% is one of the most powerful remaining tax benefits — advise salaried clients to negotiate higher employer NPS contributions as part of their CTC restructuring.
